title: Device V2 DB-derived real-device launch acceptance preparation
releaseGate: User confirms DB-derived sample scope, credential redaction boundary, clone/update strategy, collection protocols, and cleanup policy.

stories.title: DB-derived real device sample and redacted export contract
stories.scope: Inventory the current Device V2 data model, DB/API fields, credential reference fields, access point fields, and confirmed sample-selection criteria for launch acceptance. Produce a read-only sample/export contract that selects 5 real devices, preferably 1 per device type, without exposing plaintext secrets.
stories.nonGoals: Do not mutate Device V2 records, do not export plaintext credentials, do not start collection, and do not purge import batches.
acceptance: Report identifies the exact fields needed for DB-derived import: code, name, platform/status, management address or access_points, credential refs, protocol/port, labels/attributes/metadata, and original-code mapping.
Report defines candidate selection rules for 5 real devices, preferably 1 per device type, and exclusion reasons for unusable candidates.
Report documents credential redaction rules and proves no plaintext password, token, community secret, or private key is required in evidence.
Report lists the read-only command/API/query path to inspect candidates, or records the exact blocker if DB access/config is unavailable.
validation: Docs/evidence only unless a read-only backend test already exists.
Allowed backend tests: `go test ./app/device/v2/...` and `go test ./app/device/v2/ingest/...`.
Search evidence must include route/model/DTO references for Device V2, ingest, import batch, store/start, and credential reference fields.
notes: Default import strategy is cloned code D2LA_<orig_code>; same-code update is out of scope until approved.

stories.title: Frontend ingest/import template mapping for DB-derived sample
stories.scope: Map the DB-derived sample contract to the Device V2 ingest/import UI fields and Excel template. Identify any frontend gaps before real upload, including credential refs, access_points, original-code mapping, validation messages, and handoff to management.
stories.nonGoals: Do not run real import, do not redesign the UI, do not put secrets in Excel, and do not modify production data.
acceptance: Report maps DB-derived fields to ingest/import template columns and frontend API payload fields.
Report verifies UI/API support for credential_ref_in_band, credential_ref_out_band, snmp_credential_ref, winrm_credential_ref, credential_refs, and access_points where applicable.
Report defines how cloned D2LA_<orig_code> rows retain original-code traceability without overwriting originals.
Report identifies any blocker that would prevent upload/validate/apply/handoff from using the DB-derived sample.
validation: npm run typecheck:d2 if frontend source is touched.
No real upload is required in this batch.
notes: Coordinate with D2LA-001 output; do not invent sample secrets.

stories.title: Launch acceptance harness plan with fine-grained store phases
stories.scope: Extend the existing launch acceptance plan so the next batches can generate a DB-derived Excel, upload it through UI, compare imported records with source samples, then run store in separate handoff-driven phases: readiness, start, summary/runs, observations, latest DC2, collection plans, retry decision, and retry execution.
stories.nonGoals: Do not run collection against real devices in this preparatory batch, do not delete original records, and do not add broad new automation dependencies.
acceptance: Plan names the exact evidence artifacts needed for DB-derived import, management reconciliation, store readiness, store start, task summary/runs, observations, latest DC2, collection plans, retry, edit/delete, and cleanup.
Plan separates preparatory dry-run checks from real import and separates real collection into small phases that can each finish within one worker turn.
Plan defines compact handoff files between store phases, including sample-set, readiness result, task_id, summary, runs, observation classification, latest DC2, and retry decision.
Plan defines stop conditions for missing credential refs, unreachable devices, long-running tasks, empty observations/DC2, and destructive actions.
Plan keeps D2P2 local fixture smoke as a fallback health gate but does not treat it as real-device proof.
validation: Docs/evidence only unless small script inspection is needed.
If script code changes are made, run npm run typecheck:d2 and explain why no real collection was run.
notes: This story prepares Batch 002/003; it should not perform real upload or collection without user confirmation.
Store must not be implemented as one coarse story. One story should handle one device and one store phase unless the user explicitly approves a broader batch.
